content.thinking: Good, I can see the issues. Now let me apply all three fixes.

content.text: The pod is stuck because of the wrong PVC name. Now I'll apply all three fixes.

input.command:
# Fix 1: History Server PVC reference
kubectl patch deployment spark-history-server -n spark-history --type=json \
  -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/template/spec/volumes/0/persistentVolumeClaim/claimName", "value": "spark-history-pvc"}]' 2>&1

# Fix 2: History Server log directory
kubectl set env deployment/spark-history-server -n spark-history \
  SPARK_HISTORY_OPTS="-Dspark.history.fs.logDirectory=/mnt/spark-logs -Dspark.history.fs.update.interval=5s" 2>&1

# Fix 3: Team A RoleBinding namespace
kubectl patch rolebinding spark-role-binding -n spark-team-a --type=json \
  -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/subjects/0/namespace", "value": "spark-team-a"}]' 2>&1

content.content:
deployment.apps/spark-history-server patched
deployment.apps/spark-history-server env updated
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/spark-role-binding patched

tool_use_result.stdout:

deployment.apps/spark-history-server env updated

content.text: All three patches applied. Now I'll wait for the history server to come up and the jobs to complete.

input.command:
# Wait for history server pod to be running
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get pods -n spark-history -l app=spark-history-server --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $3}')
  echo "[$i] History server status: $STATUS"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "Running" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 5
done

input.command:
# Wait for both SparkPi jobs to complete
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  A=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-a -n spark-team-a --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $2}')
  B=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-b -n spark-team-b --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $2}')
  echo "[$i] team-a: $A | team-b: $B"
  if [ "$A" = "1/1" ] && [ "$B" = "1/1" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll both SparkPi jobs until complete
